element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to follow criminal vehicle while managing time limit
Correct turns required to maintain pursuit efficiency; wrong turns extend chase time
Criminal must be rammed multiple times to force stop and arrest
Initial time extended to allow ramming phase; player must complete ramming before extension expires
Each level features different criminal with distinct vehicle type and characteristics

typical_relief_triggers: Completing ramming sequence and forcing arrest
typical_tension_triggers: Time limit countdown during pursuit phase
Crashes or slow driving causing criminal escape risk
Incorrect road splits extending pursuit time
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: High-intensity pursuit cycles with escalating tension as time limit approaches; relief triggered by interception, then tension reapplied during ramming phase

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to pursuit mechanics and time management with moderate time allowances (60-70 seconds per phase)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased criminal vehicle complexity and road navigation challenges through branching paths
difficulty_curve.late_game: Eastern Bloc Spy final level with higher vehicle performance and compressed time margins
notable_spikes: First branching path encounter requiring route knowledge
Level 5 (Eastern Bloc Spy) featuring unidentified red Porsche 928 with matching player vehicle specifications

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard level flow: radio briefing on criminal identity and vehicle → pursuit phase with time limit → interception → ramming phase with extended time → arrest and level completion
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Ralph, Idaho Slasher in White Lotus Esprit)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each criminal operates progressively more sophisticated vehicle; final level mirrors player vehicle (Porsche 928) creating mechanical parity
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 5 (Eastern Bloc Spy in Red Porsche 928)

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple time limit phases: initial pursuit deadline and secondary ramming deadline; both use 60/65/70 second windows to regulate pacing

sources_used: wikipedia (Chase H.Q. article - gameplay section and SNES release information)
tcrf (Super Chase H.Q. SNES page - debugging features and release dates)
